signals.headline: Inflation Rose 3
signals.raw:
Inflation Rose 3.3% Year-over-Year; Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady at April 28 Meeting**[2] | YES | HIGH confidence
Recent CPI data showing 3.3% inflation over the past 12 months has solidified market expectations for no rate change, with traders pricing a 97% chance of a hold rather than cuts.[2] This is confirmed and would move prediction markets by removing rate-cut expectations.
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Fed Chair Powell Warns Trump's Tariffs Will Cause 'Higher Inflation And Slower Growth'**[1] | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Fed Chair Powell Warns Trump's Tariffs Will Cause 'Higher Inflation And Slower Growth'**[1] | YES | HIGH confidence
This statement from Powell directly addresses the tariff-inflation connection and would likely move markets by reinforcing expectations of sustained inflation pressure.

signals.headline: Cavs Win Game 1 Over Raptors in Conference Semifinals | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Cavs Win Game 1 Over Raptors in Conference Semifinals | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors to take a 1-0 series lead in their conference semifinals matchup, with fans reacting to the dominant win as a strong start toward the Eastern Conference Finals.[3]

signals.headline: Cooper Flagg Drops 45 Points in Mavs' Win Over Injury-Depleted Lakers | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Cooper Flagg Drops 45 Points in Mavs' Win Over Injury-Depleted Lakers | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Cooper Flagg scored 45 points, 9 assists, and 8 rebounds to lead the Dallas Mavericks to a 134-128 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers, ending Dallas' 32-year home losing streak; this follows his 51-point game and bolsters his NBA Rookie of the Year case amid a tight race.[1]
matched_markets: Will Cooper Flagg win the 2025–26 NBA Rookie of the Year award?
